expressions.notes: 빠지다 = to fall out (of something); to fall in (to something); to drown; to be dropped; to be hooked

눈 = eye
눈이 멀다 = to be blind; to go blind

뭐 = what; something
뭐 is the contracted form of 무엇.

expressions.english: Tell me about your first love.
expressions.notes:
이야기 = story; what one says
이야기해 하다 = to talk, speak, say, tell; to have a conversation; to tell a story
-이/어/여 주세요. = (polite) Please do + something + for me.

expressions.english: I have someone that I love now.
expressions.notes: 생기다 = to come into being; to be created; to appear; to be formed

expressions.english: Do you say the words "I love you" a lot?
expressions.notes:
말 = language; what one says; expression; word; term
말을 하다 = to speak; to talk; to tell; to say
많다 = to be a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.

expressions.english: They say women become more attractive when they fall in love.
expressions.notes:
여자 = girl, woman
예쁘다 = to be pretty
-이/어/여지다 = to become + adjective

expressions.english: Kyeong-hwa confessed that she loves me.
expressions.notes: -다고 = just because; saying that + S + V

expressions.english: There are many love stories in song lyrics.

expressions.korean: 사랑하는 제 동생이 이번 여름에 결혼을 해요.
expressions.english: My beloved brother is getting married this summer.
expressions.notes:
동생 = younger sister/brother
이번 = this; this time
여름 = summer
결혼을 하다 = to get married

expressions.korean: 이 세상에서 저는 저희 부모님을 가장 사랑해요.
expressions.english: (Out of all the people) In this world, I love my parents the most.
expressions.notes:
이 = this
세상 = world
저희 = (polite) we; us; our
부모님 = parents
가장 = the most

expressions.korean: 사랑하는 사람 때문에 종교를 바꾸는 사람도 있어요.
expressions.english: There are people that convert religions because of the people they love.
expressions.notes:
때문에 = because (of), since
종교 = religion
바꾸다 = to change
있다 = to be there; to exist; to have
